requirements.task_name: Copy a Node.js code sample for creating a project with a specific name
requirements.task_description: Navigate to POST /projects, switch to Node.js, change project name to 'Sample Project A', and copy the modified Node.js code sample.

requirements.task_name: Use the Try-It console to list users created after a specific date with a fixed page size
requirements.task_description: Configure and execute a GET /users Try-It request with created_after=2023-01-01 and per_page=25.

required_variables.set_condition: Immediately before returning, whenever executeTryItRequest is called for an endpointVersion whose path is '/users' and whose effective HTTP method (methodOverride or endpointVersion.httpMethod) is 'get'.
required_variables.value_to_set: An object like { endpointVersionId, method, path, fullUrl, queryParams, headers, bodyJson }, where: endpointVersionId is endpointVersion.id; method is the final HTTP method used (lowercase, e.g. 'get'); path is endpointVersion.path with path params substituted; fullUrl is the constructed baseUrl + path + query string (as already placed in X-Debug-Try-It-Url); queryParams is the array of {name, value} passed in; headers is the array of {name, value} passed in; bodyJson is body.rawJson (or null if not provided).
required_variables.reason: This object lets the evaluator verify that the user executed a GET /users Try-It request with created_after set to '2023-01-01' and per_page set to '25', and that the request was actually sent via the console.

requirements.task_name: Compare order details endpoint behavior between v1 and v2 API versions
requirements.task_description: Open GET /orders/{id}, view v1 parameters, then switch to v2 and locate the 'include' query parameter that supports 'items'.

required_variables.set_condition: Whenever getApiEndpointVersionDetails is called and the resolved endpointVersion has path === '/orders/{id}' and httpMethod === 'get' (case-insensitive).
required_variables.value_to_set: An object of the form { endpointId, viewedVersionIds, lastViewedVersionId }, where: endpointId is endpointVersion.endpointId; viewedVersionIds is an array of unique endpointVersion.id values that have been requested for this endpoint (append the current id if not already present); lastViewedVersionId is set to the current endpointVersion.id.
required_variables.reason: The evaluator can check that both the v1 and v2 version IDs for GET /orders/{id} appear in viewedVersionIds, proving that the user switched between them. Because the code synthesizes the 'include' parameter for v2, viewing v2 details is sufficient to know that the v2 'include: items' behavior was visible.

requirements.task_name: Identify the documented rate limit per minute for the payments creation endpoint
requirements.task_description: Navigate to the rate limiting docs, find the table entry for POST /payments, and focus on the per-minute rate limit value.

required_variables.set_condition: During the mapping over filtered rules in getRateLimitRules, if a rule r produces a result entry where primaryHttpMethod (lowercased) === 'post' and pathTemplate contains '/payments' (or equals '/payments'). This will occur when the rate limits table that includes POST /payments is constructed.
required_variables.value_to_set: An object such as { ruleId, primaryHttpMethod, pathTemplate, perMinuteLimit }, where: ruleId is r.id (or null if not present); primaryHttpMethod is the effective method for the rule (as already computed); pathTemplate is the effective path for the rule (as already computed); perMinuteLimit is taken from r.perMinute if present, otherwise r.limitPerMinute if present, otherwise null.
required_variables.reason: This records that the POST /payments rule was part of the rendered rules and exposes the per-minute limit value so the evaluator can verify that the user accessed the correct row and its documented rate limit.

requirements.task_name: Open and copy the example payload for the 'invoice.paid' webhook event
requirements.task_description: Find the 'invoice.paid' webhook event, view the JSON payload example, and copy it with the copy button.

requirements.task_name: Locate the Python SDK method for deleting a customer by ID
requirements.task_description: Open the Python SDK docs, navigate to the Customers module, and open the method example that deletes a customer using a customer_id argument.

required_variables.set_condition: Whenever getSdkMethodDetails is called and a method is successfully resolved (i.e., enrichedMethod is non-null).
required_variables.value_to_set: An object like { methodId, methodName, sdkLanguage, moduleId }, where: methodId is method.id; methodName is method.name; sdkLanguage is the SDK language resolved via the method's module and sdk (e.g., 'python' when the user is viewing the Python SDK); moduleId is method.moduleId.
required_variables.reason: The evaluator can verify that the lastViewedSdkMethod corresponds to the Python Customers delete method (by comparing methodId/methodName and sdkLanguage), which demonstrates that the user successfully located the correct delete-customer-by-ID example.

requirements.task_name: Copy a cURL example that uses API key authentication in the Authorization header
requirements.task_description: In the Authentication docs, navigate to the API key section and copy the cURL example with an Authorization: Bearer <api_key> header.

requirements.task_name: Configure a paginated request for pending orders using the Try-It console
requirements.task_description: Execute a GET /orders Try-It request with status=pending, per_page=50, page=3, and sort=-created_at.

required_variables.set_condition: Immediately before returning, whenever executeTryItRequest is called for an endpointVersion whose path is '/orders' and whose effective HTTP method (methodOverride or endpointVersion.httpMethod) is 'get'.
required_variables.value_to_set: An object like { endpointVersionId, method, path, fullUrl, queryParams, headers, bodyJson }, with the same structure as task2_lastTryItRequest: endpointVersionId is endpointVersion.id; method is the final HTTP method used; path is endpointVersion.path with path parameters applied; fullUrl is the constructed URL (already exposed via X-Debug-Try-It-Url); queryParams is the array of {name, value} actually sent; headers is the array of {name, value} sent; bodyJson is body.rawJson or null.
required_variables.reason: This allows the evaluator to confirm that the user executed a GET /orders request via the Try-It console with status='pending', per_page='50', page='3', and sort='-created_at'.
